Description & Notes: Full-service website design and build. Platforms include Webflow, WordPress, Next.js, and React — chosen based on client needs. Covers marketing sites, corporate sites, portfolio sites, and multi-page business websites. Platforms are mentioned as capabilities on the service page, not listed as separate services.

Online stores with product catalogs, payment gateway integration, inventorm CRM integrations, payment processors, data syncing between tools, webhook setups, and middleware. Often paired with automation services.

Connecting tools and eliminating repetitive manual tasks using n8n, Make, or Zapier. Email sequences, data syncing, report generation, lead routing, internal operations. The core promise: things that used to take hours now run on autopilot.

Custom AI assistants for customer support, lead capture, internal knowledge bases, and multi-agent systems. Built using Claude, ChatGPT, or open-source models depending on the use case. Includes conversational design, training, and deployment.

Embedding AI capabilities into existing business tools and workflows. Adding intelligence to CRMs, email systems, content pipelines, analytics, and customer interactions. Not about replacing tools — about making existing tools smarter.

Automating non-marketing operations: HR onboarding flows, invoice processing, inventory alerts, reporting dashboards, data entry elimination. For clients who need operational efficiency, not just marketing automation.

For businesses that know AI matters but don't know where to start. Includes AI readiness assessments, tool audits, roadmap creation, and implementation planning. This is the entry-point conversation that often leads to integration and automation projects.

Site audits, Core Web Vitals optimization, schema markup, crawl budget management, site architecture, indexation fixes, and page speed. The foundation layer — everything else in SEO depends on this being solid.

Google Business Profile optimization, local citations, map pack rankings, review management, and location-based content strategy. For clients serving specific geographic areas — insurance brokers, service businesses, retail, clinics.

Pillar-cluster content architecture, topical authority building, keyword research, content planning, and editorial calendars. Not just writing blog posts — building a content system that compounds organic traffic over time.

Getting brands surfaced in AI-powered search — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews. Includes entity optimization, llms.txt implementation, structured data, and content formatting for AI retrieval. Also covers Generative Engine Optimization (GEO). This is where the market is heading and most agencies aren't here yet.

Full-funnel lead acquisition — paid ads, landing page strategy, lead magnets, funnel design, and lead scoring. Combines multiple channels into one system that captures, qualifies, and routes leads automatically. Often the first engagement with startup clients.

Campaign design, nurture sequences, behavioral triggers, lifecycle emails, and deliverability optimization. Built on Resend API or client's existing ESP. Includes Outlook-compatible HTML email development with VML fallbacks.

Selecting, setting up, and automating the right CRM for the client's business. HubSpot, Salesforce, and industry-specific CRMs like PowerBroker, Acturis, Applied Epic. Includes pipeline design, lead scoring rules, and automated follow-up workflows.

Making existing traffic and leads convert better. A/B testing, UX audits, form optimization, landing page improvements, and analytics setup. For clients who already have traffic but aren't converting enough of it.

Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads — managed campaigns with reporting and optimization. Listed separately because clients specifically search for ad management. Strategy, creative, targeting, bid management, and performance reporting.

Strategy, content calendar, scheduling, and community management across LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and X (Twitter). LinkedIn is primary focus (~65%) given B2B positioning. Includes platform-specific formatting, character limits, and posting cadence.

Thought leadership content, blog posts, content calendars, brand voice development, and editorial direction. From ideation to publishing. Includes competitive content analysis and positioning to ensure content stands out in the market.

AI-powered content pipelines — DigiiMark's differentiator in this category. Multi-agent systems where AI researches topics, generates drafts, creates visuals, and schedules posts with human approval gates. Powered by Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and n8n workflows.

Infographics, social media graphics, brand identity design, presentation design, and visual storytelling. Light backgrounds, minimal text, single-insight focus. Includes brand guidelines, logo design, and creative direction for clients who need a cohesive visual identity.
